title: Aureline dashboard-truth view and freshness cards
description: Boundary schema for the dashboard-truth view and freshness cards minted by `crates/aureline-shell/src/dashboard_truth/model.rs`. One view record is rendered identically across the service-health dashboard, review inbox, incident queue, support queue, and admin queue on desktop shell, CLI / headless inspect, diagnostics, and support exports. Each contained card pins one row: its declared headline state (displayed_state), the freshness of the data behind it (freshness), the age of the last-successful evidence (evidence_age), the kind and canonical ref of the durable object its open-details affordance routes to (evidence_kind, evidence_ref), the derived honest state (effective_state), and the precise downgrade reasons. The no-silent-green invariant is structural: effective_state is `clear` only when displayed_state is `clear`, freshness is `fresh`, and evidence_age is current (`fresh` or `recent`); any other combination on a would-be-green row becomes `unconfirmed`, sets green_downgraded, and lights honesty_marker_present. Queue surfaces additionally carry a queue_order record (queue_order_reason.schema.json). Raw endpoint URLs, hostnames, credentials, raw payloads, raw operator identity strings, and absolute paths never cross this boundary; surfaces carry opaque object refs, stable tokens, and short reviewable sentences only.

dashboard_truth_view_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for the view payload. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump.

dashboard_freshness_card_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for a card payload. Card-level additive-minor changes bump this integer.

short_title.description: Short row title. No raw paths, identity strings, or payloads.

reviewable_sentence.description: Short user-facing sentence safe to render across shell, CLI/headless inspect, diagnostics, and support exports.

iso_date_time_minutes.description: ISO 8601 UTC date-time accurate to the minute. The model parses YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM and ignores any seconds, fractional, or trailing Z suffix when computing evidence-age buckets.

canonical_object_ref.description: Canonical durable-object ref of the form aureline://<class>/<id>. The <class> segment MUST be a specific object class, never a generic landing page (home, dashboard, landing, index, overview, start, root); the builder rejects those.

displayed_state_token.description: Declared headline state reported by the source before the freshness/evidence rules apply.

effective_state_token.description: Derived honest headline. `clear` is only reachable from a declared-clear, fresh, evidence-current row; `unconfirmed` is the no-silent-green downgrade.

freshness_token.description: How current the data behind the row is, as declared by its source. Anything other than `fresh` is a downgrade the chrome must surface.

evidence_age_token.description: Age bucket of the last-successful evidence relative to as_of. `fresh` and `recent` are current; `stale`, `very_stale`, and `never` cannot back a green claim.

evidence_kind_token.description: Closed vocabulary of canonical durable-object classes the evidence ref points at.

downgrade_reason_token.description: Closed vocabulary explaining a freshness/evidence downgrade. Empty for a fresh, evidence-current row.

last_successful_evidence_at.description: Timestamp of the last-successful evidence, or null when none has been captured (evidence_age is then `never`).

green_downgraded.description: True when displayed_state was `clear` but effective_state was withdrawn to `unconfirmed`.

honesty_marker_present.description: True when the chrome must light a yellow honesty chip on this row.

queue_order.description: Present for queue surfaces (review_inbox, incident_queue, support_queue, admin_queue); null for the service_health dashboard.
